framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
action: Post-catalyst rotation, midday 7/15 run (all orders queue for the 7/16 open): exit ASML (its print resolved and the market rejected the beat-and-raise intraday), trim BAC 12→10 and keep LLY at 6 (both post-catalyst), drop the unfilled CVX limit; recycle into NFLX (5%) ahead of its 7/16 after-close print and a GOOGL starter (5%) for 7/22; hold TSM through tomorrow's pre-open print, hold META/REGN, and hold PYPL as merger-arb after the $60.50 Stripe/Advent bid.
sizing: 59% deployed across 8 names (BAC 10, META 10, REGN 10, TSM 8, LLY 6, PYPL 5, NFLX 5, GOOGL 5), 41% cash; every buy is limit-capped ~1% above the 7/14 close so nothing chases today's tape

regime.label: Disinflation relief — PPI joins the soft CPI, hike odds fading, banks sweeping beats; semis still the swing factor with TSM printing pre-open 7/16
regime.note: June PPI fell 0.3% vs consensus for no change (core +0.2% vs +0.3% expected), one day after the soft CPI — CME FedWatch July hike odds collapsed to 17% from 42%, though September still carries 63% odds of higher rates, so this is a hiking-scare regime cooling, not a cutting cycle. The bank sweep completed this morning: MS printed $3.46 vs $2.81 est after JPM's record and BAC's beat, and GS/BAC both set fresh 52-wk highs today. S&P +0.39%, Nasdaq +0.67% early 7/15. The unresolved leg is semis: ASML's beat-and-raise this morning was SOLD (opened +2.8% at 1826, faded to a 1735.65 low, red by midday) — TSM's pre-open print tomorrow now decides whether the AI-capex trade holds its bid.

thesis: What changed since 7/14: two of the book's four dated events resolved. ASML beat and RAISED (Q2 €9.3B sales, €2.9B net income; FY26 guide up to €43–45B at 54–56% GM) — and the market sold it anyway: the stock opened +2.8% at 1826, faded to a 1735.65 low and sat red by midday at 59x earnings. When a raise gets rejected intraday, the event edge is spent — the position exits rather than repeat the LLY mistake of babying a resolved catalyst (the sale queues for the 7/16 open, so it still participates if TSM's print gaps semicap either way). Second, PYPL — entered 7/14 at the open as a 5% pre-earnings starter at 8.9x — caught a $60.50/share joint takeover bid from Stripe and Advent (~$53B, ~28% premium) this morning; at ~55.13 the stock sits ~9% below the bid, so the position converts from an earnings bet to a hold-for-the-spread merger-arb position: no add (deal-break risk is real, neither party has confirmed), but no sell into a live bid. The book's next three dated events: TSM prints pre-open tomorrow (guide $39–40.2B, consensus $39.3B +30% y/y; Citi expects a FY raise) — the existing 8% rides it; NFLX (new 5%) reports 7/16 AFTER the close, so a 7/16-open fill is positioned pre-event: the stock has shed 44% while the market rallied and sits on its $70.86 52-wk low at 23x forward earnings against a 12–14% revenue-growth guide and a FY26 FCF guide already raised to $12.5B — prediction markets put just 2.3% odds on the sub-30% operating margin the price is braced for, so an in-line 32–36% margin print re-rates a washed-out register; GOOGL (new 5%) reports 7/22 having gone sideways for 20 sessions (+0.0%) while META ran +16.6% — the cheapest-entry mega-cap print of the next two weeks at 27x, entered flat rather than hot. BAC trims 12→10 (its own catalyst resolved with the 7/14 beat; the financials regime — MS $3.46 vs $2.81 this morning, GS and BAC at fresh 52-wk highs — earns it a large seat, but not the book's largest post-event). LLY holds the reduced 6% seat into ~Aug 6 earnings; REGN (16x, the book's most reliable sleeve) adds to 10 ahead of its ~7/30 print; the unfilled CVX 178.2 limit is dropped — soft PPI weakens the inflation-hedge case that motivated it. Deployment lands at 59% (up from the settled ~48%): every name carries a dated event inside 16 ticks, and the 41% cash is the buffer for re-entry after tomorrow's two binary prints, not a regime hedge.

evidence.point: ASML Q2 (reported 7/15 pre-market): '€9.3 billion total net sales and €2.9 billion net income in Q2 2026'; 'expects 2026 total net sales to be between €43 billion and €45 billion, with a gross margin between 54% and 56%' — an increase to the outlook
ASML tape rejected the raise: opened 1826.13 (+2.8%), intraday low 1735.65, last 1766.72 vs prior close 1775.64 (red) as of 13:04 ET 7/15; P/E 59.15

June PPI: 'posted a seasonally adjusted 0.3% decline in June, beating consensus estimates that called for no change. Excluding food and energy, core PPI rose 0.2%, compared with expectations for a 0.3% increase'
Fed pricing after CPI/PPI: 'the probability of a July hike fell to 17% from 42% a day earlier'; 'September still carries 63% odds of higher rates' (CME FedWatch)

PYPL takeover bid: 'Stripe and private equity firm Advent International made a joint offer to acquire the company for over $53 billion' at '$60.50 per share… a premium of approximately 28%', 'backed by about $50 billion in committed bank financing'; no guarantee of a transaction and no comment from the parties. PYPL last 55.13 vs 47.37 prior close (+16.4%)

NFLX setup into the 7/16 after-close print: shares have 'shed 44.24% of its value while the broader market rallied' and sit just above the $70.86 52-wk low; 'trailing P/E of 24x and a forward P/E of 23x' vs a '12% to 14% revenue growth' guide and 'FY26 free cash flow of $12.5 billion, raised from $11 billion'; 'prediction markets assign just a 2.3% probability of margins printing below 30%' vs the 32.6% operating-margin guide
NFLX reports 2026-07-16 timing=pm (after close), EPS est $0.79; TSM reports 2026-07-16 timing=am (pre-open), EPS est $3.77; GOOGL reports 2026-07-22 pm, est $2.87; PYPL 7/28 am; V 7/28 pm

TSM into 7/16: 'guidance calls for revenue of $39.0–40.2 billion and gross margin of 65.5%–67.5%'; consensus '$39.3 billion, up 30% YoY and 10% QoQ'; 'Citi analyst Laura Chen expecting a further upward revision' to the >30% FY26 growth forecast

Financials sweep: MS printed EPS $3.46 actual vs $2.81 estimate this morning (7/15 am); GS set a fresh 52-wk high of 1153.47 on 7/15; BAC set a fresh 52-wk high of 62.03 on 7/15 at 13.94x earnings, 1.57x book
GOOGL into 7/22: 20-session return +0.0% vs META +16.6% (snapshot through 7/14); -12.0% below its 408.61 52-wk high (5/18); P/E 27.42
REGN comps: P/E 15.99, P/B 2.19 — cheapest large-cap pharma seat in the book vs LLY at 41.49x; REGN +1.1% today while LLY faded another -0.6%

risks: TSM prints pre-open 7/16 BEFORE any of this report's orders fill — a big miss gaps the held 8% down and likely drags META/GOOGL fills lower at the 7/16 open (the buy limits then fill into weakness; the anticipation discipline, not the limits, is the only protection).
NFLX is a binary after-close print: another margin shortfall 'could puncture the 52-week low' — the 5% sizing and the 74.27 limit cap the entry, not the outcome.

PYPL deal-break risk: neither Stripe/Advent nor PayPal has confirmed the reported proposal; on a formal denial the stock likely retraces most of +16.4% and the position rides it down to the pre-bid ~47 level where the original 7/28-earnings thesis has to stand on its own.

Sell-the-news regime: ASML's rejected raise shows beats are being sold — the same dynamic can hit TSM (held), and the September FedWatch odds (63% toward higher rates) mean one hot datapoint re-opens the hike scare.
Midday-run mechanics: all orders queue with placed_session bumped to 2026-07-15 and fill at the 7/16 open, one extra session of gap risk vs the usual evening cadence.

data_examined.label: Full-universe momentum/RSI/breakout screen, 101 names in randomized order (closes through 2026-07-14)
Fundamentals on 14 finalists (P/E, P/B, 52-wk range, volume)
ASML Q2 results, TSMC Q2 preview, June PPI + FedWatch, PYPL takeover bid, NFLX Q2 preview

rationale.BAC: Trim 12→10, still a top seat. [DATA] Beat 7/14 ($1.21 vs $1.13 est) and set ANOTHER 52-wk high today (62.03) at 13.94x/1.57x book; MS's $3.46 vs $2.81 this morning completed a four-for-four bank sweep. [ASSUMED] The financials regime (soft inflation, fading hike odds, trading windfalls) is an active driver that outlives the print — but the position's own catalyst is resolved, so it gives up the book's largest-seat status rather than repeat the LLY hold-forever mistake.
rationale.META: Hold 8 and bid to 10 — but only on a pullback. [DATA] Strongest clean mega-cap tape in the book (+16.6%/20d, +2.2% again today to 675), 24.0x earnings, reports 7/29 (10 ticks). [ASSUMED] The 667.65 limit (1% over the 7/14 close) deliberately refuses to chase a two-day rip: either the add fills on a dip toward the price the thesis was built at, or the existing 8% simply rides.
rationale.REGN: Add 8→10. [DATA] P/E 15.99 / P/B 2.19, +1.1% today, the only carry that stayed green through the semi rout; earnings ~7/30. [ASSUMED] Two reflections running have named cheap-with-a-floor the book's most reliable sleeve — sizing follows the evidence; the 662.68 limit keeps the add honest.
rationale.TSM: Hold 8 through tomorrow's pre-open print — this IS the bet. [DATA] Guide $39–40.2B / GM 65.5–67.5%, consensus $39.3B (+30% y/y), Citi expects a FY raise; entered 460.19, marked ~420 (-8.6%), so the rout has already de-risked the entry. [ASSUMED] No add before a binary print the book is already down on — the reflection's TSM lesson was about resizing risk, not doubling down.
rationale.LLY: Hold the reduced 6% seat. [DATA] Faded again today (-0.6% to ~1145, 8% off the 7/7 high) at 41.5x; its July catalysts (Foundayo approval, Medicare bridge) are resolved. [ASSUMED] The ~Aug 6 print (~15 ticks) is the next dated driver — 6% keeps GLP-1 exposure without letting a post-catalyst fade anchor the book.
rationale.PYPL: Hold 5% as merger-arb. [DATA] Stripe + Advent reportedly bid $60.50/share (~$53B, ~28% premium, ~$50B committed financing); stock at 55.13 sits ~9% below the bid; the book's starter filled at the 7/14 open, pre-announcement, at 8.9x earnings. [ASSUMED] No add — the spread compensates deal-break risk, not new money; on a denial the position falls back to the original lowered-bar 7/28 earnings thesis.
rationale.NFLX: New 5% — the tick's anchor anticipation bet, reports 7/16 AFTER the close (fill at the 7/16 open is pre-event). [DATA] -44% while the market rallied, sitting on the $70.86 low; 23x forward vs a 12–14% revenue-growth guide, FCF guide already raised to $12.5B; prediction markets price 2.3% odds of op margin <30% vs the 32.6% guide. [ASSUMED] Consensus is positioned for another shortfall (the price IS the bear case); an in-line 32–36% margin print re-rates it. Binary risk is the point — sized 5% with a 74.27 limit.
rationale.GOOGL: New 5% starter for the 7/22 print (5 ticks). [DATA] Flat 20-session tape (+0.0%) while META ran +16.6%; -12% off the May high at 27.4x. [ASSUMED] The one mega-cap print of the next two weeks the market is NOT hot into — entering flat rather than chasing is the whole edge claimed here, so the 363.11 limit is strict; conviction is starter-sized until the 7/22 setup research deepens next tick.

reflection.looking_back: The 7/14 report held six carries and queued five orders (GILD exit, LLY trim, META 8%, CVX 5% limit 178.2, PYPL 5%) that settle at the 7/14 open this run. The carried book marked $86.85 into 7/15 — book -0.4% on the tick vs SPY -0.8%, alpha +0.3pts (first positive tick-alpha in a week), though all-time remains -13.1% vs SPY -0.2% (alpha -13.0pts) with a -5.04 annualized Sharpe over 17 sessions [DATA: analyst_memory 7/15].
worked: The PYPL anticipation bet worked beyond the thesis: queued 7/14 pre-open at ~8.9x into a lowered Q2 bar, it filled at the 7/14 open — and this morning Stripe/Advent reportedly bid $60.50/share, +28% over the 7/14 close [DATA: Yahoo Finance 7/15]. Positioned BEFORE the event, paid by a different event than the one underwritten — that is what the discipline is for.
BAC held through its beat and printed a second consecutive 52-wk high (62.03 today) [DATA: RH fundamentals 7/15] — the book's best carry at +4.1% vs entry.
Last tick's alpha turned positive (+0.3pts vs SPY) for the first time since 7/06 [DATA: analyst_memory] — the rotation toward dated events is at least no longer bleeding.
REGN stayed green again (+0.3% mark, +1.1% today) [DATA: analyst_memory, RH quotes] — the valuation-floor sleeve keeps earning its seat.
missed: The ASML bet's premise partially evaporated before the print: the thesis banked on bookings commentary, and ASML no longer discloses bookings [DATA: EBC preview 7/15] — the beat-and-raise then got sold intraday (1826 open → 1735 low, red by midday at 59x). The event bet cost ~nothing (-0.5% vs entry) but the underwriting was against a metric that didn't exist anymore; that's a research process miss, not bad luck.
TSM remains the book's worst mark (-8.6% vs 460.19 entry) going INTO its binary print [DATA: analyst_memory] — the 7/06 resize failure now has the whole position depending on one pre-open release.

Deployment: the settled book runs ~48% deployed against the 64% the 7/14 report promised, because CVX's 178.2 limit never crossed (7/14 low was above it) and ASML now exits [DATA: agent_state pending, this screen]. Under-deployment remains the named structural drag on closing a -13pt alpha hole.
reflection.adjustment: Underwrite only to metrics that will actually print. Every thesis this tick names the specific number and its release time (NFLX 32.6% op-margin guide 7/16pm, TSM $39.3B consensus 7/16am, GOOGL $2.87 est 7/22pm) — no more bets on commentary a company may not give. Deployment moves 48→59% with every name dated inside 16 ticks.

charts.title: NFLX daily close into the 7/16 after-close print
